title: Finding disposition and repair-authority flow
goal: Make actionable findings and out-of-authority repairs end in explicit dispositions or replan proposals, while preserving invalid worker-output attempts as durable evidence before retry.
acceptance: Worker and repair outputs that contain actionable findings must give each finding a terminal disposition or an associated pending replan proposal before the run can be advertised as handoff-ready.
Out-of-area or workflow-policy repair needs are converted into proposal evidence instead of being applied silently by a repair worker.
Accepting a repair/finding proposal applies only the approved revision or follow-up; rejecting or deferring preserves the finding, rationale, and evidence in status/report/handoff surfaces.

Invalid worker output is preserved as a durable attempt artifact/event before retry, including the raw invalid payload or bounded parse error, attempt number, slice id, and transcript/output tails where available.
Runtime economics and attempt history count invalid-output attempts accurately instead of making the retry look like a single successful agent call.
The HERDR/FEED invalid-output evidence gap is covered by regression tests: invalid JSON/schema output preserves evidence, while check-failed attempts continue preserving their existing artifacts.
must_ask_if: The implementation would allow repair workers to mutate workflow policy, profiles, verification commands, slice contracts, dependencies, or paths outside authorized areas without an operator-approved proposal.
The implementation would drop or overwrite invalid worker-output evidence during retry.
The implementation would let workers approve their own evidence or mark actionable findings resolved without a disposition/proposal.
The implementation would broaden worker output authority beyond the existing JSON Issue Slice fence.
